task: #43 P9.C12 deeper Q4 selected-dual repack/layout path

memory_model.note: P9.C5 measured side metadata as slower regardless of cache size. This artifact recomputes actual qwen35moe bytes from GGUF metadata (expert_ffn=512). Final runtime repack must replace raw allocation, not duplicate all Q4 gate/up weights, because duplicating raw-size repack for all MoE layers would add ~8.4 GiB.

rejected_designs.hot_fulltile_plus_fallback: Correct but Q4 replay 65-66 ms vs retained 59.9 ms; hot full-tile alone ~49 ms but fallback ~17.5 ms.
rejected_designs.fp16_scale_min_sidecar: Correct but first real layer 2.339 ms vs raw 1.678 ms; extra side stream lost despite removing bitfield decode.
rejected_designs.tail_no_padding: Residual rows only 5.4%; measured tail fallback cost exceeded padding gain.

candidate_layouts.name: Q4T16 tile-major replacement layout (selected v1)
candidate_layouts.description: Repack each Q4_K gate/up tensor from raw [E,out,row_bytes] into tile-major [E,out_tile16,k_block] slabs. Within each slab, store d/dmin fp16 per output column, predecoded scale/min uint8 per subblock/column, and q4 nibbles arranged by [subblock, kt, k_lane16, col] so a wave loading a 16-column B fragment reads contiguous/coalesced bytes instead of 16 strided raw GGUF rows. Final runtime should store this as the resident allocation replacing raw for Q4 gate/up; replay prototype may allocate one-layer side buffers.
expected_kernel_changes: new qweight_tile pointer ABI for Q4 selected dual only
load d/dmin and uint8 scale/min from contiguous column vectors
load q nibbles by k-lane/column tile instead of raw GGUF q pair stride
keep compact selected output ABI unchanged
support retained 32x16 by consuming two adjacent Q4T16 slabs per wave or initially one 16x16 slab for lower risk
go_no_go: synthetic CPU decode of Q4T16 equals raw GGUF Q4_K exactly
first qwen layer gate+up <=1.35 ms (>=20% faster than 1.678 ms raw) before full runtime wiring
full Q4 replay <=45 ms to continue; <=35 ms to be a plausible #27 target closer

candidate_layouts.name: Q4H16 half-expanded B fragments (rejected for v1)
candidate_layouts.description: Pre-expand every q4 block to fp16 or int8 B fragments. It would minimize kernel decode but roughly doubles or quadruples weight bytes and cannot replace raw within the 24 GiB budget without broad loader changes. Keep only as a microbenchmark upper-bound if Q4T16 is inconclusive.

candidate_layouts.name: Expert-major persistent selected-MoE scheduler (defer)
candidate_layouts.description: Avoid per-layer row/tile fallback overhead by persistent expert blocks and compact tail lists. P9.C9 shows tail alone is not enough without a faster weight layout, so defer until a Q4T16-like layout proves a faster hot kernel.
candidate_layouts.decision: dependency after P9.C15 if repack is insufficient

selected_v1.prototype_scope: Build host materializer from GGUFReader raw bytes for one or more layers; add CPU roundtrip tests and device-copy support in replay. Do not change default runtime until kernel improves.
selected_v1.runtime_integration_rule: If retained, materialization must provide a new allocation kind (e.g. q4_k_tile16_dual) and dispatch by registry variant; avoid backend/quant if-branches in core runtime. It should replace raw Q4 gate/up allocations under a model-load option rather than duplicate them for all layers.
